title: Fix full vulnerable controller + router + endpoint end to end
prompt:
Consider this mini stack — a router, endpoint, and controller — where each layer has one vulnerability. Rewrite all three: ```elixir # lib/my_app_web/endpoint.ex defmodule MyAppWeb.Endpoint do use Phoenix.Endpoint, otp_app: :my_app @session_options [ store: :cookie, key: "_my_app_key", signing_salt: "hardcoded-salt", http_only: false, secure: false ] plug Plug.Session, @session_options plug MyAppWeb.Router end # lib/my_app_web/router.ex defmodule MyAppWeb.Router do use MyAppWeb, :router pipeline :browser do plug :accepts, ["html"] plug :fetch_session plug :put_secure_browser_headers, %{"content-security-policy" => "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'"} end scope "/", MyAppWeb do pipe_through :browser get "/search", SearchController, :search end end # lib/my_app_web/controllers/search_controller.ex defmodule MyAppWeb.SearchController do use MyAppWeb, :controller def search(conn, %{"q" => q}) do results = MyApp.Search.run(q) html(conn, "

Results for #{q}

" <> Enum.join(results, "
")) end end ``` Five issues to fix: (1) hardcoded signing_salt — move to runtime.exs reference, (2) http_only/secure false → true, (3) unsafe-inline CSP → `default-src 'self'`, (4) controller `html/2` with interpolation → render template, (5) `plug :protect_from_forgery` missing from browser pipeline. Produce a SINGLE fixed file `lib/my_app_web/router.ex` that contains the router with all router-side fixes. The router must contain `plug :protect_from_forgery`, a CSP without `'unsafe-inline'`, and NOT have `get "/search"` leading to the html/2 version.
tier_rationale: Hard — five vulnerabilities spread across three layers. Tests ability to audit the stack.
